evals.prompt: I want to build a real-time collaborative document editor like Google Docs. It needs to support 10k concurrent users, have offline mode, and work on web and mobile. Where do I even start?
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator should identify this as a complex multi-team greenfield project requiring: (1) real-time-architect for real-time collaboration patterns (CRDTs, OT), (2) system-architect for overall system design, (3) frontend-architect for the web editor, (4) mobile-architect for mobile clients, (5) backend-architect for the collaboration server, (6) database-architect for document storage. It should propose a sequential-then-parallel execution plan: start with real-time-architect + system-architect for architecture, then parallel frontend/mobile/backend development, then QA and DevOps. Should NOT attempt to design the system itself.

assertions.name: defines-execution-sequence
assertions.description: Should define a clear execution order — architecture first, then parallel development, then testing/deployment

assertions.name: does-not-design-system
assertions.description: Should NOT attempt to design the system itself

evals.prompt: Our Node.js API is throwing 500 errors on the /users/profile endpoint after we deployed the new avatar upload feature yesterday. The logs show 'ENOMEM' errors. We're running on ECS with 512MB containers.
evals.expected_output: Route to sre-engineer as lead for incident triage with backend-architect as secondary. Should identify this as an incident response pattern. Should NOT diagnose the issue itself.

assertions.name: identifies-incident-pattern
assertions.description: Should recognize as incident response scenario

assertions.name: does-not-diagnose
assertions.description: Should NOT diagnose the memory issue itself

evals.prompt: We're a 3-person startup building a SaaS invoicing tool for freelancers. We need multi-tenancy, Stripe billing integration, PDF invoice generation, and a simple dashboard. What's our tech stack and how should we architect this?
evals.expected_output: Route to saas-architect + system-architect. Calibrate to startup scale. Should NOT pick the tech stack itself.

assertions.name: startup-scale-awareness
assertions.description: Should calibrate to startup scale

assertions.name: does-not-pick-tech-stack
assertions.description: Should NOT make tech stack decisions itself

evals.prompt: How do I set up Prometheus and Grafana to monitor my Kubernetes cluster?
evals.expected_output: Route directly to sre-engineer with minimal overhead. No multi-team plan needed.

assertions.name: minimal-overhead
assertions.description: Should route directly without multi-team coordination

assertions.name: does-not-explain-prometheus
assertions.description: Should NOT explain Prometheus setup itself

evals.prompt: We're preparing for SOC 2 Type II audit next quarter. We need to review our entire stack — the React frontend handles PII in forms, the Go backend processes payments, we use PostgreSQL and Redis, everything runs on AWS EKS. What's the plan?
evals.expected_output: Hub-and-spoke with security-engineer leading. Multiple spoke teams for stack review. Flag technical-writer for documentation.

assertions.name: identifies-spoke-teams
assertions.description: Should identify teams for each stack component

assertions.name: hub-and-spoke-pattern
assertions.description: Should use hub-and-spoke coordination

evals.prompt: I need to add a chat feature to our existing e-commerce platform. Users should be able to message sellers in real-time, with typing indicators, read receipts, and message history.
evals.expected_output: Domain-augmented development with real-time-architect + e-commerce-architect + core dev teams. Recognize existing system context.

assertions.name: identifies-dev-teams
assertions.description: Should identify backend + database + frontend teams

assertions.name: recognizes-existing-system
assertions.description: Should recognize this is adding to an existing system

evals.prompt: We need to add Stripe subscription billing to our SaaS platform. Users can upgrade/downgrade plans, we need webhook handling for payment events, and usage-based metering for API calls. Our stack is Next.js frontend, Go backend, PostgreSQL database. Team of 12 engineers.
evals.expected_output: Should classify as Tier 3 or 4. Should create a plan artifact with phase gates. Should mandate security-engineer (payment + API changes) and fintech-architect (financial flows). Should identify saas-architect for billing patterns. Plan should start at Design gate, not jump to implementation.

assertions.name: creates-plan-artifact
assertions.description: Should create a plan artifact (.etyb/plans/ or Claude plan annotation) with phase gates

assertions.name: starts-at-design-gate
assertions.description: Should enter the Design gate first, not jump to implementation

evals.prompt: Add a password reset feature to our app. It needs to send email verification links that expire after 24 hours. We have an Express.js backend with Passport.js auth and a React frontend.
evals.expected_output: Should classify as Tier 3. Should mandate security-engineer because it touches auth (login, session, tokens). Should NOT skip security review even though it seems like a simple feature.

assertions.name: identifies-auth-sensitivity
assertions.description: Should recognize this touches auth and mandate security review at Design and Verify gates

assertions.name: creates-plan
assertions.description: Should create a plan artifact for this Tier 3 request

assertions.name: does-not-skip-security
assertions.description: Should NOT treat this as a simple feature that bypasses security review

evals.prompt: I'm in plan mode working on a new user onboarding flow. The plan file is at .claude/plans/user-onboarding.md. I need to figure out the database schema, API endpoints, and frontend components. We're a growth-stage startup with 15 engineers.
evals.expected_output: Should detect Claude plan mode and annotate the existing plan rather than creating a separate .etyb/plans/ file. Should add gate status, expert assignments, and verification checkpoints to the Claude plan.

assertions.name: detects-plan-mode
assertions.description: Should detect that the user is in Claude plan mode

assertions.name: annotates-claude-plan
assertions.description: Should annotate the existing Claude plan rather than creating a .etyb/plans/ file

assertions.name: adds-gate-status
assertions.description: Should add gate status and expert assignments to the plan

assertions.name: growth-scale-calibration
assertions.description: Should calibrate to growth scale (15 engineers)

evals.prompt: We've been trying to fix a flaky integration test for 2 days. The test for the order creation endpoint passes locally but fails in CI about 40% of the time. We've tried adding retries, increasing timeouts, and resetting the database between runs — nothing works.
evals.expected_output: Should activate the debugging protocol. Should recognize this as a 3+ failure scenario requiring escalation. Should follow the debugging loop: reproduce, hypothesize, test one variable, verify. Should NOT suggest more retries or timeouts.

assertions.name: activates-debugging-protocol
assertions.description: Should activate the debugging protocol for persistent test failures

assertions.name: recognizes-escalation
assertions.description: Should recognize multiple failed attempts and apply the 3-failure escalation rule

assertions.name: hypothesis-driven
assertions.description: Should follow hypothesis-driven debugging, not shotgun debugging

assertions.name: does-not-suggest-retries
assertions.description: Should NOT suggest retries or timeouts (already tried, treats symptoms not causes)

evals.prompt: We're building a patient portal for a hospital. We need to display lab results, allow appointment scheduling, and enable secure messaging between patients and doctors. The system needs to integrate with the hospital's Epic EHR system via HL7 FHIR APIs. Budget is tight — we're 4 engineers.
evals.expected_output: Should classify as Tier 4 and create a full plan artifact. Should mandate healthcare-architect (healthcare data, HIPAA) AND security-engineer (healthcare + PII). Should detect that despite being a small team, compliance requirements demand full process rigor for healthcare data. Should NOT skip gates for the auth/data/compliance aspects even at startup scale.

assertions.name: creates-full-plan
assertions.description: Should create a full plan artifact with all phase gates

assertions.name: hipaa-compliance-awareness
assertions.description: Should recognize HIPAA requirements and not relax security gates despite small team size

assertions.name: startup-with-compliance
assertions.description: Should calibrate to startup scale but maintain full process rigor for compliance-sensitive work

evals.prompt: We just finished the Design gate for our new API. The system-architect documented the architecture and the security-engineer completed the threat model. Now the user wants to jump straight to implementation and skip the Plan gate because 'we already know what to build'.
evals.expected_output: Should enforce the Plan gate and NOT allow skipping it. Should explain that the Plan gate is where task breakdown, test strategy (by qa-engineer), and risk register happen. Should offer scale-appropriate alternatives but not skip the gate entirely.

assertions.name: blocks-gate-skip
assertions.description: Should NOT allow skipping the Plan gate

assertions.name: explains-plan-gate-value
assertions.description: Should explain what the Plan gate provides (task breakdown, test strategy, risk register)

assertions.name: offers-alternatives
assertions.description: Should offer scale-appropriate alternatives (e.g., lightweight plan) rather than a hard no

evals.prompt: Fix the typo in our README — it says 'Auhentication' instead of 'Authentication'.
evals.expected_output: Should classify as Tier 0 — trivial. Should just fix it directly with zero process overhead. No plan, no gates, no expert mandating, no routing to specialists.

assertions.name: classifies-tier-0
assertions.description: Should classify as Tier 0 trivial request

assertions.name: no-process-overhead
assertions.description: Should NOT create a plan, invoke gates, or route to specialists

assertions.name: just-fixes-it
assertions.description: Should just fix the typo directly
